personalInfo.personalStatement: Distinguished Professor of Art History with over 35 years of scholarly excellence specializing in Renaissance and Baroque art, with particular focus on Italian painting and patronage networks.

workExperience.employer: Sapienza University of Rome, Department of History, Anthropology, Religions, Art and Performing Arts
University of Bologna, Department of the Arts
Princeton University, Department of Art and Archaeology
Yale University, Department of the History of Art
The Courtauld Institute of Art, University of London
Harvard University, Department of History of Art and Architecture

workExperience.description: Italian Renaissance Art (undergraduate survey, 150–200 students); Baroque Art in Europe (undergraduate, 80–100 students); Caravaggio and Caravaggism (graduate seminar, 15–20 students); Methods and Theories in Art History (graduate, 20–25 students); Dissertation Seminars for PhD students; Museum Studies Practicum (graduate)
History of Italian Painting 1400–1600; Women Artists in Early Modern Europe; Patronage and Collecting Practices
Taught courses in Italian Renaissance and Baroque art, iconography, historiography
Caravaggio and His Legacy – Visited by 850,000+ people
Numerous exhibitions at major museums worldwide

education.description: Dissertation: "Patronage and Power: The Medici Court and Artistic Production in Late 16th Century Florence". Supervisor: Prof. Roberto Longhi (posthumously), Prof. Mina Gregori
education.institution: Scuola Normale Superiore di Pisa, Italy
education.qualificationTitle: Dottorato di Ricerca (PhD) in Art History

education.description: Summa cum laude. Thesis on Caravaggio's early Roman period.
education.institution: Università degli Studi di Roma "La Sapienza"

other.content: Italian Renaissance and Baroque painting (15th-17th centuries); Caravaggio and Caravaggism; Artistic patronage and collecting practices; Cultural exchange between Italy and Northern Europe; Gender and art in early modern Italy; Conservation and technical art history; Reception history and historiography of Italian art; Sacred and profane imagery in Counter-Reformation Rome

- Rossi, I. (ed.) (2021). *Caravaggio and His Followers in Rome*. Exhibition catalogue, Galleria
Borghese/Louvre. Officina Libraria, 456 pp.
- Rossi, I. & Schmidt, H. (2018). *The Age of Caravaggio*. Exhibition catalogue, Metropolitan
Museum of Art. Yale University Press, 380 pp.
- Rossi, I. (2015). *Artemisia Gentileschi: Power and Passion*. Exhibition catalogue, Palazzo
Braschi, Rome. Skira, 320 pp.
- Multiple catalogue essays for major exhibitions at Uffizi Gallery, National Gallery London, Prado
Museum (1990-2024)

Over 80 contributed chapters and encyclopedia entries including contributions to Grove Art Online,
Oxford Art Online, and major edited volumes on Renaissance and Baroque art.

Doctoral Students - Completed: 34

Selected recent completions:
- Dr. Marco Bellini (2017-2022): "Caravaggio's Northern Followers" - Now Associate Professor,
University of Amsterdam
- Dr. Laura Ferretti (2014-2020): "Women Collectors in 17th Century Rome" - Now Curator,
Galleria Nazionale d'Arte Antica
- Dr. Hans Mueller (2013-2019): "Artistic Exchange Netherlands-Italy" - Now Senior Lecturer,
University of Leiden
- Dr. Elena Bianchi (2012-2018): "Sacred Art in Counter-Reformation" - Now Associate Professor,
University of Padua
- Dr. James Foster (2010-2016): "Caravaggio's Technique and Materials" - Now Conservation
Scientist, Getty Conservation Institute

Current PhD Students: 3
MA/Laurea Magistrale Students: Supervised over 120 thesis projects (1986-2020)

Editorial Boards:
- Editor-in-Chief, *Arte Lombarda* (2010-2018)
- Editorial Board, *Art Bulletin* (2005-2015)
- Editorial Board, *Renaissance Studies* (2000-2012)
- Editorial Board, *Journal of the History of Collections* (1998-2010)
- Advisory Editor, *Burlington Magazine* (2012-Present)
- Editorial Advisory Board, *Artibus et Historiae* (1995-Present)

Professional Leadership:
- President, International Association of Art Historians (CIHA) (2012-2016)
- Vice President, Comité International d'Histoire de l'Art (2008-2012)
- Board Member, College Art Association (2004-2010)
- President, Renaissance Society of America (2002-2004)

Committee Service:
- Chair, Prix Balzan Selection Committee for Art History (2018-2020)
- Member, Getty Foundation Council (2014-2020)
- Member, Kress Foundation Board of Trustees (2010-2018)
- Numerous PhD examination committees at universities worldwide
- Grant review panels for NEH, ACLS, Mellon Foundation, Kress Foundation

Extensive reviewing for major publishers (Yale, Princeton, Cambridge, Chicago, Oxford University Presses) and journals. Over 400 manuscript reviews. External examiner for PhD theses at 25+ universities internationally.

Over 200 conference presentations and invited lectures at major universities and museums
worldwide (1982-2024). Recent keynote addresses include:
- Renaissance Society of America Annual Meeting (2023)
- College Art Association Annual Conference (2022, 2015, 2008)
- European Association of Art Historians Congress (2021)
- International Congress of the History of Art (2016, 2008, 2000)
- Getty Research Institute Symposium (2019)
- Clark Art Institute Lecture Series (2018)
- National Gallery of Art, Washington - A. W. Mellon Lectures (2014)

media: Consultant and on-screen expert for BBC documentary series *The Art of Italy* (2023)
Featured in Netflix documentary *Caravaggio: Into the Shadows* (2021)
Regular contributor to Italian cultural programs on RAI (2000-present)
Numerous interviews for major newspapers and magazines: *The New York Times*, *The Guardian*, *Financial Times*, *Il Corriere della Sera*, *La Repubblica*
Public lectures at major museums attended by thousands
TEDx talk on Caravaggio and modern visual culture (2019, 1.2M views)
